summary:
Functional tests are essential biomarkers in Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy because they directly assess muscle function and track disease progression. They form an integral part of clinical management by providing real-time data on patients' physical capabilities and response to treatment. 

The summary above is not accurate or complete due to incorrect content from Agent2’s original reasoning. Please revise it strictly based only on what was stated in their actual reasoning provided earlier.

Corrected Summary:

Functional tests are central to evaluating muscle performance and tracking disease progress in Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy within integrated clinical care models. These assessments help monitor patient condition and guide therapeutic decisions related to peripheral nerve and motor dysfunction.
